title: WSL2 machine: missing `/var/run/docker.sock`
body:
### Issue Description

Podman machine on WSL2 doesn't have `/var/run/docker.sock`.  Podman machine on Hyper-V  has it.
`podman-docker` package is installed on WSL2 machine.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

**Hyper-V**
```
PS C:\Users\user> $env:CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER="hyperv"
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine init --now
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh ls -ld /var/run/docker.sock
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 33 Feb  7 18:19 /var/run/docker.sock -> /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

**WSL2**
```
PS C:\Users\user> $env:CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER="wsl"
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine init --now
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh ls -ld /var/run/docker.sock
ls: cannot access '/var/run/docker.sock': No such file or directory
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh rpm -qa podman-docker
podman-docker-4.9.0-1.fc39.noarch
```

**Work around.**
```
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh sudo dnf reinstall podman-docker -y
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh ls -ld  /var/run/docker.sock
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 33 Feb  8 03:34 /var/run/docker.sock -> /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

**Effects of this bug.**
Unable to run VS Code Dev Container. (e.g. Dev Volumes(clone from Git Repo) and Try a sample.)
Because, Dev Container needs to mount `docker.sock` in bootstrap processes.
VS Code hardcodes `/var/run/docker.sock` and cannot handle this on the user side.
```
(Dev Container's log)
...
[91472 ms] Cloning Github repository: Microsoft/vscode-remote-try-python into /workspaces/vscode-remote-try-python

[91472 ms] Start: Run: podman run -d --mount type=volume,src=vscode-remote-try-python-57162747f0204512b2a2119607d864e61e3cfb714da0e7ce0cf5751654850e99,dst=/workspaces -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock vsc-volume-bootstrap sleep infinity
[91767 ms] Command failed: podman run -d --mount type=volume,src=vscode-remote-try-python-57162747f0204512b2a2119607d864e61e3cfb714da0e7ce0cf5751654850e99,dst=/workspaces -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock vsc-volume-bootstrap sleep infinity
[91767 ms] Error: statfs /var/run/docker.sock: no such file or directory
[91767 ms] Exit code 125
```

### Describe the results you received

```
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh ls -ld /var/run/docker.sock
ls: cannot access '/var/run/docker.sock': No such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
PS C:\Users\user> podman machine ssh ls -ld /var/run/docker.sock
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 33 Feb  7 18:19 /var/run/docker.sock -> /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

PS C:\Users\user> podman version

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This issue continues in Podman v5.0.0.
As of the [podman-machine-wsl-os](https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os) image, docker.sock does not exist.
I think the process of adding docker.sock is necessary when creating the WSL machine.

This issue is not reproduced on a new Windows environment(clean installed Windows).
It seems to be probably an issue in my environment.
I solved the problem, following the steps below. (I inspired [this comment](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/8996#issuecomment-1326840801))

Inside Podman machine
```
rm /dev/shm
mount --bind -o X-mount.mkdir /run/shm /dev/shm
```
